sections.content:
**Property Address:** 667 Bear Cove Rd, Deer Harbor, WA 98243

**Status:** Documented

### Generator Instructions
![Generator Instructions](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/FdqOlUIjWfQvyDzB.jpg)

**Steps to switch to generator power:**
1. Start generator (Located behind Camp Kitchen Shelter)
2. Turn off House Main Switch #1
3. Turn off main Switch on panel #2
4. Switch #3 Transfer Switch to Gen. set
5. Turn on House Main Switch #1
6. Turn on Main Switch on panel #2

**To switch back to OPALCO power:**
1. Turn off House Main Switch #1
2. Turn off main Switch on panel #2
3. Switch #3 Transfer Switch to OPALCO
4. Turn off generator
5. Turn on House Main Switch #1
6. Turn on Main Switch on panel #2

### Critical Contacts
- **Emergency:** 911
- **Caretaker (Joseph):** 360-982-9705
- **Caretaker (Lilliana):** 360-317-6121
- **Power (OPALCO):** 360-376-3500

sections.title: Workflow Guide
sections.content:
# How to Add Photos to the Property Manual (Android)

**Simple Step-by-Step Guide for Adding Photos from Your Phone**

---

## What You Need

- Your Android phone with photos
- The property manual website link
- Internet connection

---

## The Process (Super Simple Version)

### Step 1: Take Your Photos

Walk around the property and take photos of whatever you want to document (water heaters, electrical panels, valves, etc.). Don't worry about organizing them yet—just take the pictures.

### Step 2: Open the Property Manual Website

On your phone or computer, open this link:

**https://3000-ij5bpzk4aks2mnrac8550-bd361b2c.us2.manus.computer/photo-labeling**

This is the photo upload page.

### Step 3: Upload Your Photos

**On your phone:**
- Tap the gray upload box
- Select "Choose Files" or "Browse"
- Pick all the photos you want to upload (you can select multiple at once)
- Wait for them to load (you'll see them appear numbered: Photo #1, Photo #2, etc.)

**On your computer:**
- Drag and drop the photos into the gray box, OR
- Click "Select Files" and choose them from your folders

### Step 4: Label Each Photo

For each photo, type a label in the text box below it. Use this format:

**[Building/System] - [What It Is] - [Where It Is]**

**Examples:**
- `Main House - Water Heater - Utility Room`
- `Farm House - Electrical Panel - Garage`
- `Irrigation System - Valve Box A - Front Yard`
- `Well System - Pressure Tank - Pump House`

**Tips:**
- If you don't remember what a photo is, just skip it (leave the box empty)
- Photos without labels will be deleted automatically
- Your progress saves automatically—you can close the page and come back later

### Step 5: Export Your Labels

When you're done labeling (or need to take a break):
1. Click the **"Export Labels"** button at the top
2. A text file will download to your phone/computer
3. Send that file to me (Manus) in the chat

### Step 6: I Take Over

Once you send me the labels file:
- I'll upload all the photos to the cloud (CDN)
- I'll organize them into the right sections of the manual
- I'll update the website so you can see them
- I'll save a checkpoint

**That's it!** You take the photos, label them, and I handle the rest.

---

## Quick Reference

| Step | What You Do | Where |
|------|-------------|-------|
| 1 | Take photos with your phone | Out in the field |
| 2 | Open photo upload page | https://3000-ij5bpzk4aks2mnrac8550-bd361b2c.us2.manus.computer/photo-labeling |
| 3 | Upload photos | Tap/click upload box |
| 4 | Label each photo | Type: Building - Item - Location |
| 5 | Export labels | Click "Export Labels" button |
| 6 | Send me the file | In Manus chat |

---

## Common Questions

**Q: Can I upload photos from my phone's camera roll?**  
A: Yes! You can upload any photos from your phone, whether you just took them or they're from weeks ago.

**Q: How many photos can I upload at once?**  
A: As many as you want! The page can handle hundreds of photos.

**Q: What if I forget to label a photo?**  
A: No problem—just leave it blank. I'll automatically skip any photos without labels.

**Q: Can I edit labels after I export them?**  
A: Yes! Just open the text file and edit the labels before sending it to me.

**Q: What if I close the page before I'm done?**  
A: Your progress saves automatically. Just reopen the same link and your labels will still be there.

**Q: Do I need to organize photos into folders first?**  
A: Nope! Just upload them all at once and label them. I'll organize them into the right sections of the manual.

---

## Need Help?

If you get stuck, just send me a message in the chat and I'll walk you through it!

### Maintenance
- **Annual Service:** Oil furnace requires annual cleaning and tune-up
- **Filter Changes:** Check/replace air filters quarterly
- **Oil Tank:** Monitor heating oil levels (see Water Shutoff section for oil tank access photo)
- **Safety:** Carbon monoxide detector recommended near furnace

### Thermostat

![Main House - Living Room Thermostat](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/mNTpurljOdZDKnbI.jpg)

**Brand:** Honeywell
**Type:** Programmable digital thermostat
**Location:** Living room wall
**Display:** LCD touchscreen with current temperature, set point, and time
**Controls:** Up/down temperature adjustment buttons, programmable schedule buttons
**Current Reading:** 69°F (room temperature), Set to 60°F

**Features:**
- 7-day programmable schedule
- Digital touchscreen interface
- Precise temperature control
- Battery backup for settings

**Programming:**
- Press "Program" button to access schedule settings
- Set different temperatures for Wake, Leave, Return, Sleep periods
- Adjust individual days or copy schedule across week
- Hold button for temporary override

**Maintenance:**
- Replace batteries annually (typically 2 AA batteries)
- Clean display with soft, dry cloth
- Check accuracy with separate thermometer annually
- Verify furnace responds to temperature changes

### Bedroom Thermostat (Basement Furnace Zone)

![Main House - Bedroom Thermostat](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/CWDTDSJPUCvmKSxI.jpg)

**Brand:** Honeywell
**Type:** Programmable digital thermostat
**Location:** Bedroom wall
**Heating Zone:** Basement furnace (separate from living room zone)
**Display:** LCD showing current hold temperature
**Current Reading:** Hold at 60°F
**Controls:** Set Clock/Day, Set Schedule, Time adjustment (Ahead/Back), Temperature adjustment (Warmer/Cooler), Run Program, Hold Temp

![Bedroom Thermostat - Programming Instructions](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/lVifIdiHZZXmIiGI.jpg)

**Features:**
- Programmable heating/cooling schedules
- Temporary temperature override
- Hold function for extended temperature settings
- Fan control (On/Auto)
- System mode switch (Cool/Off/Heat)

**Programming Instructions (from flip-up panel):**
- **TIME/DAY:** Press "Set Clock/Day", use TIME buttons to adjust, press "Set Clock/Day" again to confirm
- **HEATING PROGRAM:** Press "Set Schedule", adjust with WAKE/Leave/Return/Sleep periods using TIME and TEMP buttons
- **TEMPORARILY change temperature:** Press WARMER or COOLER (returns to program at next scheduled period)
- **HOLD temperature:** Press "Hold Temp" to maintain current setting (press "Run Program" to resume schedule)
- **CURRENT temperature setting:** Displayed on screen

**Multi-Zone Heating System:**
The Main House has two separate heating zones:
1. **Living Room Zone:** Controlled by living room thermostat → Side entry oil-fired furnace
2. **Bedroom Zone:** Controlled by bedroom thermostat → Basement furnace

**Maintenance:**
- Replace batteries annually (typically 2 AA batteries)
- Clean display with soft, dry cloth
- Verify both zones heat independently
- Check that basement furnace responds to bedroom thermostat changes

### Basement Furnace (Bedroom Zone)

![Main House - Basement Exterior Entrance](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/nOlUgGlqnWamrwnw.jpg)

**Location:** Basement (accessed via exterior stone foundation entrance)

![Basement Furnace Closet](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/PZTOXOInIoLwdRWg.jpg)

**Access:** Beige door with top and bottom ventilation grilles

![Thermo Pride Basement Furnace](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/cPXdYMWTssiofMsY.jpg)

**Brand:** Thermo Pride
**Type:** Oil-fired forced air furnace
**Cabinet:** White/beige with copper-colored side panels
**Fuel:** Heating oil
**Ductwork:** Black flue pipe visible, metal supply/return ducts
**Heating Zone:** Bedroom zone (controlled by bedroom thermostat)
**Location:** Basement furnace closet

![Thermo Pride Installation Manual](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/nNVtGHbyxYqVcqBw.jpg)

**Manufacturer:** Thermo Products Inc., Box 217, North Judson, IN 46366, Phone: (219) 896-2133

**Safety Warnings:**
- Improper installation, adjustment, alteration, service, or maintenance can cause injury or property damage
- Do not store or use gasoline or other flammable vapors and liquids in vicinity of this or any other appliance
- Refer to manual for proper installation and maintenance
- Must be serviced by qualified installer or service agency

**Complete Multi-Zone Heating System:**

The Main House has **two independent oil-fired forced air furnaces**:

1. **Living Room Zone**
   - Furnace: Side entry utility room (Thermo Pride SSO-100)
   - Thermostat: Living room Honeywell programmable
   - Coverage: Main living areas

2. **Bedroom Zone**
   - Furnace: Basement (Thermo Pride, model TBD)
   - Thermostat: Bedroom Honeywell programmable
   - Coverage: Bedroom areas

Both furnaces share the same heating oil supply tank.

**Maintenance:**
- Annual professional service before heating season
- Quarterly filter replacement (both furnaces)
- Monthly visual inspection of flue pipes
- Monitor oil tank level regularly
- Test both zones independently
- Keep basement furnace closet ventilation grilles clear
- Check for oil leaks around both units

subsections.content:
- **Brand:** Bradford White
- **Model:** ERE350S6-1NCWW
- **Serial:** ZC51064910
- **Type:** Electric storage water heater
- **Capacity:** 50 US gallons (189.2 liters)
- **Location:** Side entry utility room
- **Voltage:** 240V / 208V (1-phase AC only)
- **Wattage:** Upper Element 4500W / 3500W (50/60 Hz), Lower Element 4500W / 3500W, Maximum 4500W / 3500W
- **Pressure:** Test 300 psi, Working 150 psi
- **Features:** HydroJet technology, AHRI Certified

### Water Heater Photos

![Main House - Bradford White Water Heater - Side Entry](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/dWPhENDQysYhUXDn.jpg)
**Main House - Bradford White Water Heater - Side Entry**

![Main House - Water Heater Specifications Label](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/QYszUUPXgzKGzyvR.jpg)
**Main House - Water Heater Specifications Label**

### Maintenance
- **Annual Inspection:** Check for leaks, corrosion, and proper operation
- **Drain & Flush:** Annually to remove sediment buildup
- **Temperature:** Test water temperature (recommended 120°F to prevent scalding)
- **Pressure Relief Valve:** Test annually to ensure proper operation
- **Anode Rod:** Inspect every 3-5 years, replace if corroded
- **Expected Lifespan:** 10-15 years for electric water heaters

### Basement Water Heater

![Main House - Basement Water Heater](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/ekNgfITDCIHjxfOY.jpg)

**Location:** Basement (near electrical panel)

![Basement Water Heater Specification Label](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/zlOjxBCtTUCzKeqS.jpg)

**Brand:** Bradford White Corporation
**Model:** ERE250T6-1NCWW
**Serial:** BB54805614
**Capacity:** 50 US gallons (189.2 liters)
**Type:** Electric storage tank water heater
**Voltage:** 240V, 1 Phase AC ONLY
**Upper Element:** 4500W, 50/60 Hz
**Lower Element:** 4500W Maximum
**Pressure Rating:** Test 300 psi, Working 150 psi
**DR-ready:** CTA-2045-A (demand response capable)
**Certification:** UL Listed E14356 (Household Storage Tank Water Heater), AHRI Certified

**Dual Water Heater System:**

The Main House has **two separate 50-gallon Bradford White electric water heaters**:

1. **Side Entry Water Heater**
   - Model: ERE350S6-1NCWW
   - Serial: ZC51064910
   - Location: Side entry utility room
   - Serves: Main house domestic hot water

2. **Basement Water Heater**
   - Model: ERE250T6-1NCWW
   - Serial: BB54805614
   - Location: Basement (near electrical panel)
   - Serves: Basement/additional zones

Both units are identical 50-gallon, 4500W dual-element electric water heaters with HydroJet technology and demand response capability.

**Maintenance (Both Units):**
- Annual inspection and drain/flush
- Test temperature and pressure relief valve quarterly
- Check water temperature (120°F recommended)
- Inspect for leaks around connections
- Replace anode rod every 3-5 years
- Check electrical connections annually
- Monitor for unusual noises or performance issues

### Main Water Shutoff Access

![Main House - Water Shutoff Access - Exterior](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/zzgcGpvmvcltjOrb.png)

**Main Water Shutoff Access Location**

Exterior access to main water shutoff valve located near white siding. Wooden plank cover on ground provides access to underground shutoff valve. Green garden hose coiled nearby for reference.

**Emergency Shutoff Procedure:**
1. Remove wooden plank cover
2. Locate shutoff valve below ground level
3. Turn valve clockwise to shut off water supply
4. Replace cover when complete

---

subsections.title: Electrical Systems
subsections.content:
### Entry Utility Room Panel #1

![Main House - Entry Utility Room Panel #1](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/XuXjzavvzZSbMCHA.jpg)

**Brand:** Pushmatic Electri-Center (Bulldog Electric Products Co., Detroit, Michigan)
**Type:** Main electrical panel
**Location:** Side entry utility room
**Breaker Type:** Pushmatic push-button breakers (vintage 1950s-1970s era)
**Age:** Vintage

**Labeled Circuits:**
- Left side: Spare, Refrigerator, Furnace
- Right side: Spare, Kitchen Outlets, Range Exhaust, Freezer

**Note:** Pushmatic breakers are obsolete technology. Replacement breakers may be difficult to source. Consider panel upgrade for improved safety and compatibility with modern electrical requirements.

### Entry Utility Room Panel #2

![Main House - Entry Utility Room Panel #2](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/asKdNbDOochNIabE.jpg)

**Brand:** Pushmatic Electri-Center (Bulldog Electric Products Co., Detroit, Michigan)
**Type:** Main electrical panel with main circuit breaker
**Location:** Side entry utility room
**Breaker Type:** Pushmatic push-button breakers (vintage 1950s-1970s era)
**Main Breaker:** Located at top of panel
**Age:** Vintage

**Labeled Circuits:**
- Left side: Dryer, Unknown, Water Heater, Range, Disposal
- Right side: Dryer, Laundry, Water Heater, Range, Dishwasher, Disposal and Garbage

**Safety Notes:**
- Main breaker provides whole-house disconnect capability
- Pushmatic breakers are obsolete technology
- Professional electrical inspection recommended
- Consider modern panel replacement for enhanced safety and capacity

### Basement Electrical Panels

The Main House basement contains three electrical panels providing comprehensive power distribution:

#### Panel 1: Square D QO Load Center (Modern)

![Basement Panel 1 - Square D QO Load Center](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/UDdcQCidFCXuwVhZ.jpg)

**Type:** Modern Square D QO Load Center
**Main Breaker:** Principal breaker with yellow warning label
**Circuits:**
- Main House Docks
- Boat House
**Circuit Directory:** Handwritten labels on door including "Trees Light"

#### Panel 2: Pushmatic Electri-Center (Vintage - Outdoor/Exterior Circuits)

![Basement Panel 2 - Pushmatic Outdoor Circuits](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/ivQKodYHutTtXQAW.jpg)

**Type:** Pushmatic Electri-Center (Bulldog Electric Products Co., Detroit, Michigan)
**Left Side Circuits:**
- Furnace
- Lights: Dressing Room, M.Bath, Hall to Office, Furnace Room
- Floods: Parking Side
- Hot Water

**Right Side Circuits:**
- Corner Floods on House
- Lights Path to Chalet
- Terrace/Patio Lights, Floods
- Lighting Trees (BaySide)
- Outside buzzer
- Bathrooms Fans
- Heater

#### Panel 3: Pushmatic Electri-Center (Vintage - Interior Circuits)

![Basement Panel 3 - Pushmatic Interior Circuits](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/jWjNxhnJCfbQxXcg.jpg)

**Type:** Pushmatic Electri-Center (Bulldog Electric Products Co., Detroit, Michigan)
**Left Side Circuits:**
- Kitchen Ceiling, Utility Room, Rear Porch
- Lights: Front Hall, Guest Bath, Master Shower, Room, Middle Light over M.Bathroom
- Outlets: Bill's Office, Living Room & Entrance
- Lights: Shop, Crawl Space
- Outlets: Shop
- Master Bathroom Outlets
- Sunroom Lights
- Outside Outlets

**Right Side Circuits:**
- Living Room Outlets, Spot Over Table, East Kitchen Outlets
- Bill's Office Lights & outlets
- MB Outlets, Meditation Light & outlets, Basement Outlets
- Terrace/Patio Outlets
- Trees Lights to dock, Road to Boat House
- Fire Alarm
- Outside Outlets

**Complete Main House Electrical System:**

The Main House electrical system consists of **five panels total**:

1. **Side Entry Panel 1** (Pushmatic) - Refrigerator, Furnace, Kitchen Outlets, Range Exhaust, Freezer
2. **Side Entry Panel 2** (Pushmatic with main breaker) - Dryer, Water Heater, Range, Disposal, Laundry, Dishwasher
3. **Basement Panel 1** (Modern Square D QO) - Main House Docks, Boat House
4. **Basement Panel 2** (Pushmatic) - Outdoor/exterior lighting and floods, furnace, water heater, fans
5. **Basement Panel 3** (Pushmatic) - Interior lighting, outlets, fire alarm

**Safety Notes:**
- Three of five panels use obsolete Pushmatic technology (push-button breakers)
- Pushmatic breakers are no longer manufactured; replacement parts difficult to source
- Consider professional electrical inspection and potential panel upgrade
- Modern Square D panel (Panel 1) meets current electrical codes
- Ensure all panel doors remain accessible and unobstructed
- Label any unlabeled circuits for future reference
- Test GFCI/AFCI protection regularly if installed

**Breaker Type:** Pushmatic push-button breakers (vintage 1950s-1970s era)
**Age:** Vintage

**Labeled Circuits:**
- Left side: Spare, Refrigerator, Furnace
- Right side: Spare, Kitchen Outlets, Range Exhaust, Freezer

**Note:** Pushmatic breakers are obsolete technology. Replacement breakers may be difficult to source. Consider panel upgrade for improved safety and compatibility with modern electrical requirements.

### Entry Utility Room Panel #2

![Main House - Entry Utility Room Panel #2](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/asKdNbDOochNIabE.jpg)

**Brand:** Pushmatic Electri-Center (Bulldog Electric Products Co., Detroit, Michigan)
**Type:** Main electrical panel with main circuit breaker
**Location:** Side entry utility room
**Breaker Type:** Pushmatic push-button breakers (vintage 1950s-1970s era)
**Main Breaker:** Located at top of panel
**Age:** Vintage

**Labeled Circuits:**
- Left side: Dryer, Unknown, Water Heater, Range, Disposal
- Right side: Dryer, Laundry, Water Heater, Range, Dishwasher, Disposal and Garbage

**Safety Notes:**
- Main breaker provides whole-house disconnect capability
- Pushmatic breakers are obsolete technology
- Professional electrical inspection recommended
- Consider modern panel replacement for enhanced safety and capacity

### Basement Electrical Panels

The Main House basement contains three electrical panels providing comprehensive power distribution:

#### Panel 1: Square D QO Load Center (Modern)

![Basement Panel 1 - Square D QO Load Center](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/UDdcQCidFCXuwVhZ.jpg)

**Type:** Modern Square D QO Load Center
**Main Breaker:** Principal breaker with yellow warning label
**Circuits:**
- Main House Docks
- Boat House
**Circuit Directory:** Handwritten labels on door including "Trees Light"

#### Panel 2: Pushmatic Electri-Center (Vintage - Outdoor/Exterior Circuits)

![Basement Panel 2 - Pushmatic Outdoor Circuits](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/ivQKodYHutTtXQAW.jpg)

**Type:** Pushmatic Electri-Center (Bulldog Electric Products Co., Detroit, Michigan)
**Left Side Circuits:**
- Furnace
- Lights: Dressing Room, M.Bath, Hall to Office, Furnace Room
- Floods: Parking Side
- Hot Water

**Right Side Circuits:**
- Corner Floods on House
- Lights Path to Chalet
- Terrace/Patio Lights, Floods
- Lighting Trees (BaySide)
- Outside buzzer
- Bathrooms Fans
- Heater

#### Panel 3: Pushmatic Electri-Center (Vintage - Interior Circuits)

![Basement Panel 3 - Pushmatic Interior Circuits](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/jWjNxhnJCfbQxXcg.jpg)

**Type:** Pushmatic Electri-Center (Bulldog Electric Products Co., Detroit, Michigan)
**Left Side Circuits:**
- Kitchen Ceiling, Utility Room, Rear Porch
- Lights: Front Hall, Guest Bath, Master Shower, Room, Middle Light over M.Bathroom
- Outlets: Bill's Office, Living Room & Entrance
- Lights: Shop, Crawl Space
- Outlets: Shop
- Master Bathroom Outlets
- Sunroom Lights
- Outside Outlets

**Right Side Circuits:**
- Living Room Outlets, Spot Over Table, East Kitchen Outlets
- Bill's Office Lights & outlets
- MB Outlets, Meditation Light & outlets, Basement Outlets
- Terrace/Patio Outlets
- Trees Lights to dock, Road to Boat House
- Fire Alarm
- Outside Outlets

**Complete Main House Electrical System:**

The Main House electrical system consists of **five panels total**:

1. **Side Entry Panel 1** (Pushmatic) - Refrigerator, Furnace, Kitchen Outlets, Range Exhaust, Freezer
2. **Side Entry Panel 2** (Pushmatic with main breaker) - Dryer, Water Heater, Range, Disposal, Laundry, Dishwasher
3. **Basement Panel 1** (Modern Square D QO) - Main House Docks, Boat House
4. **Basement Panel 2** (Pushmatic) - Outdoor/exterior lighting and floods, furnace, water heater, fans
5. **Basement Panel 3** (Pushmatic) - Interior lighting, outlets, fire alarm

**Safety Notes:**
- Three of five panels use obsolete Pushmatic technology (push-button breakers)
- Pushmatic breakers are no longer manufactured; replacement parts difficult to source
- Consider professional electrical inspection and potential panel upgrade
- Modern Square D panel (Panel 1) meets current electrical codes
- Ensure all panel doors remain accessible and unobstructed
- Label any unlabeled circuits for future reference
- Test GFCI/AFCI protection regularly if installed

sections.title: Boat House
sections.content:
**Status:** Documented

The Boat House is a dark wood structure with weathered metal roof located near the waterfront. It provides storage for marine equipment, boats, and water sports gear, with comprehensive electrical distribution serving the building interior, docks, boat ramp, and waterfront areas.

### Building Overview

![Boat House Exterior](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/hxkBSOvIZgNhHbsO.jpg)

**Structure:** Dark wood building with weathered metal roof, covered porch area, surrounded by tall evergreen trees

**Purpose:** Marine equipment storage, boat storage, water sports gear

**Features:** Interior electrical panel, dock electrical distribution, boat ramp power, waterfront outlets

### Electrical Systems

The Boat House electrical system consists of four separate distribution points serving different areas of the waterfront property.

subsections.title: Electrical Systems
subsections.content:
The Boat House electrical system provides power distribution to the building interior, Main House docks, boat house docks, and boat ramp areas. The system consists of four separate panels and outlet installations.

### Main Interior Panel

![Boat House Interior](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/xwuUvLsTtTeFUKbh.jpg)

**Location:** Interior wall, left side upon entry

**Surroundings:** White painted walls, oars/paddles mounted on wall, life jackets and marine equipment hanging, concrete floor

---

![Boat House Main Electrical Panel](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/jvNbMltcilUuthKz.jpg)

**Type:** Square D circuit breaker panel

**Circuit Directory** (yellow label-maker labels):

**Left Column:**
- DOCK

**Right Column:**
- 100 AMP MAIN
- 200 AMP MAIN
- W INCH
- WINCH
- INSIDE ROOM LIGHTS
- OUTSIDE LIGHTS 20AMPS (blue label)
- OUTSIDE OUTLET

**Panel Label:** "MAIN" written above panel

**Features:**
- Dual main breakers (100A and 200A capacity)
- Winch circuits for boat launching operations
- Interior and exterior lighting circuits
- Dock power distribution
- Outdoor outlet circuits

---

### Main House Dock Panel

![Main House Dock Walkway Panel](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/CLygQJwZBMtxbWGL.jpg)

**Location:** Mounted on post at beginning of wooden dock walkway

**Type:** Weatherproof electrical panel box (gray/green)

**Surroundings:** Water and aluminum dock ramp visible in background, moss-covered dock planks

---

![Main House Dock Panel Open](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/wrsIXFRmIPIxTAEV.jpg)

**Panel:** Square D QO Load Center

**Features:**
- Circuit breakers with warning labels
- "QO LOAD CENTER" label at top
- Mounted on white PVC conduit post
- Weatherproof enclosure for marine environment

---

![Main House Dock Outlet](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/WTNpRyMiJUmSVTAa.jpg)

**Location:** Weatherproof electrical outlet mounted on wooden dock post at water's edge

**Type:** Gray receptacle cover with orange/red outlet

**Surroundings:** Rocky shoreline and clear water visible

---

### Boat House Dock Panel

![Boat House Dock Outlet Box](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/MvlMAELgNbRofsYx.jpg)

**Location:** Mounted on wooden dock post under covered dock structure

**Type:** Weatherproof electrical outlet box with white/gray cover

**Features:** Orange toggle switch visible on cover

**Surroundings:** Calm water and rocky shoreline in background, moss-covered dock planks

---

![Boat House Dock Panel Open](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/FaUjRPQItVmKAjmc.jpg)

**Circuit Breakers:** Located at top of panel

**Outlets:**
- **Yellow twist-lock outlet** (30A marine/RV style) - for high-power marine equipment
- **Two standard black duplex outlets** (120V) - for general use

**Installation:** Mounted under covered dock structure with water visible, mesh cable protection

---

### Boat Ramp Panel

![Boat Ramp Panel Installation](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/RIMMvmUpyjhBnJAT.jpg)

**Location:** Weathered outdoor electrical panel in wooded hillside area near water

**Type:** Aged gray/blue panel boxes mounted on post

**Condition:** Visible weathering and rust, exposed to elements

**Surroundings:** Ferns and forest debris, natural hillside setting

---

![Boat Ramp Panel Open](https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/113311765/mHAOqLfCnOBlQoos.jpg)

**Features:**
- Circuit breaker at top
- Twist-lock outlet (marine equipment)
- Two standard duplex outlets below
- Heavy weathering with rust on lower junction box

**Purpose:** Power for boat ramp operations, marine equipment, and waterfront activities

---

### Electrical System Summary

**Four Distribution Points:**

1. **Main Interior Panel** - Building interior lights, winch operations, exterior lights, dock power, outlets
2. **Main House Dock Panel** - Square D QO Load Center serving Main House dock walkway and waterfront
3. **Boat House Dock Panel** - Marine-grade outlets (30A twist-lock + standard 120V) for boats and equipment
4. **Boat Ramp Panel** - Weatherproof outdoor installation for boat launching and ramp operations

**Power Capacity:**
- Main panel: Dual mains (100A + 200A)
- Specialized circuits for boat winch operations
- Marine-grade 30A twist-lock outlets for high-power equipment
- Standard 120V outlets for general use
- Dedicated dock and outdoor lighting circuits

**Safety Features:**
- Weatherproof enclosures for marine environment
- Circuit breaker protection at all distribution points
- GFCI protection recommended for all waterfront outlets
- Mesh cable protection on dock installations

**Maintenance Notes:**
- Boat ramp panel shows significant weathering and rust - monitor condition
- All waterfront electrical should be inspected annually for corrosion
- Test GFCI outlets monthly
- Keep panel doors closed when not in use to prevent moisture intrusion
- Verify all connections remain tight in marine environment
- Consider panel replacement for heavily rusted boat ramp installation
